title: Update working memory from session
summary: Durable context learned during the current coding session.

claims.text: ClickHouse session storage keeps project identity as separate raw fields: project_path, git_remote, package_name, and package_type; base session and session analytics schemas do not define a stored repository fallback column.

DeveloperDetailPage has a read-side project-name resolver that displays the last segment of git_remote with the full remote as tooltip, then package_name, then the last segment of project_path; the page uses it for the projects chart, project filter labels, and session history project cells.

DeveloperSessionSchema and DeveloperProjectSchema still expose project_path but not git_remote or package_name, so the developer detail frontend cannot receive the raw project identity fields through the shared API contract yet.

getDeveloperSessions and getDeveloperProjects still select and group by project_path without selecting git_remote or package_name, and they return a service-local project_name derived from project_path via extractProjectName.
Project identity fallback should be computed on the read path or in the frontend from git_remote, package_name, and project_path rather than during ingest.

For developer project-name resolution, repository should not be treated as the canonical display source because it represents a precomputed fallback; future work should preserve git_remote, package metadata, and project_path as separate canonical inputs.

project_path remains part of the developer project identity model for filtering and grouping, but it should be the final display-name fallback after git_remote and package_name because it is a user-local filesystem path.

Finish the developer project-identity read path by adding git_remote and package_name to DeveloperSessionSchema and DeveloperProjectSchema, selecting those fields in getDeveloperSessions, and aggregating them with any(...) in getDeveloperProjects.

After raw project identity fields are exposed for developer analytics, remove the service-local extractProjectName/project_name path fallback from developer.service.ts because display-name resolution belongs in the read/frontend fallback chain.

sources.title: Claude Code session: Developer name resolution and analytics cleanup

metadata.reason: The user explicitly corrected the initial approach, saying git_remote, project_path, and the package-derived project name should remain separate fields and fallback should happen on read or in the frontend, not during ingest.
The session reconsidered the existing repository column and identified it as a precomputed fallback that conflicted with the user's requested separate raw-field model.
The user asked whether project_path still exists, and the session clarified that project_path remains as the raw filesystem path but should not be the preferred human display label.
The updated plan created during the session lists the remaining read-side work to expose git_remote and package_name in the shared schemas and developer service queries.
The updated session plan calls out extractProjectName/project_name as a service-local display fallback that should be removed once raw project identity fields are exposed.
